section_name: Use and Alteration of Property of Another Political Subdivision
text:
(a) For a purpose described by Section 173.201 (General Authority over Commuter Rail Facilities) , as necessary or useful in the construction, reconstruction, repair, maintenance, and operation of the system, and with the consent of a municipality, county, or other political subdivision, a district may:

(1) use streets, alleys, roads, highways, and other public ways of the political subdivision; and

(2) relocate, raise, reroute, change the grade of, or alter, at the district’s expense, the construction of a publicly owned or privately owned street, alley, highway, road, railroad, electric line or facility, telegraph or telephone property or facility, pipeline or facility, conduit or facility, and other property.

(b) A district may not use or alter:

(1) a road or highway in the state highway system without the permission of the commission; or

(2) a railroad without permission of the railroad.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 5
### Chapter 173. Intermunicipal Commuter Rail Districts
##### § 173.203 — Use and Alteration of Property of Another Political Subdivision

(a) For a purpose described by Section 173.201 (General Authority over Commuter Rail Facilities) , as necessary or useful in the construction, reconstruction, repair, maintenance, and operation of the system, and with the consent of a municipality, county, or other political subdivision, a district may:

    (1) use streets, alleys, roads, highways, and other public ways of the political subdivision; and

    (2) relocate, raise, reroute, change the grade of, or alter, at the district’s expense, the construction of a publicly owned or privately owned street, alley, highway, road, railroad, electric line or facility, telegraph or telephone property or facility, pipeline or facility, conduit or facility, and other property.

(b) A district may not use or alter:

    (1) a road or highway in the state highway system without the permission of the commission; or

    (2) a railroad without permission of the railroad.

*Added by Acts 2009, 81st Leg., R.S., Ch. 85 (S.B.1540), Sec. 2.04, eff. April 1, 2011.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: For a purpose described by Section 173.201 (General Authority over Commuter Rail Facilities) , as necessary or useful in the construction, reconstruction, repair, maintenance, and operation of the system, and with the consent of a municipality, county, or other political subdivision, a district may:
use streets, alleys, roads, highways, and other public ways of the political subdivision; and

relocate, raise, reroute, change the grade of, or alter, at the district’s expense, the construction of a publicly owned or privately owned street, alley, highway, road, railroad, electric line or facility, telegraph or telephone property or facility, pipeline or facility, conduit or facility, and other property.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: A district may not use or alter:
a road or highway in the state highway system without the permission of the commission; or
a railroad without permission of the railroad.

history.raw: Added by Acts 2009, 81st Leg., R.S., Ch. 85 (S.B.1540), Sec. 2.04, eff. April 1, 2011.
